records.ety: [OF. curios, curius, F. curieux, L. curiosus careful, inquisitive, fr. cura care. See Cure.]

defs: Difficult to please or satisfy; solicitous to be correct; careful; scrupulous; nice; exact.
quotes: How shall we, If he be curious, work upon his faith?

defs: Exhibiting care or nicety; artfully constructed; elaborate; wrought with elegance or skill.
quotes: His body couched in a curious bed.

defs: Careful or anxious to learn; eager for knowledge; given to research or inquiry; habitually inquisitive; prying; -- sometimes with after or of.
quotes: It is a pity a gentleman so very curious after things that were elegant and beautiful should not have been as curious as to their origin, their uses, and their natural history.

defs: Exciting attention or inquiry; awakening surprise; inviting and rewarding inquisitiveness; not simple or plain; strange; rare.
quotes: Many a quaint and curious volume of forgotten lore.
Abstruse investigations in recondite branches of learning or sciense often bring to light curious results.
Many . . . which used curious arts brought their books together, and burned them.
